verdict_reasoning: TGN46/TGN38 is a type I integral membrane protein that predominantly resides in the trans-Golgi network but constitutively cycles between the TGN, endosomes, and the plasma membrane. A documented minority pool reaches the cell surface during the cycling itinerary (well-established by surface biotinylation and antibody-uptake assays, which is in fact how TGN38/46 trafficking was originally characterized), with the ectodomain exposed extracellularly during its brief PM transit. This qualifies as dual_localization — the protein has its own TM anchor at the PM during the surface stage, even though the steady-state dominant compartment is the TGN.
